{"score": 5.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:L"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 5.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:L"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 6.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:L/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-347", "name": "Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature"}], "description": "## Summary

Before OpenClaw 2026.3.31, the Nostr DM ingress path could issue pairing challenges before validating the event signature. A forged DM could create a pending pairing entry and trigger a pairing-reply attempt before signature rejection.

## Impact

An unauthenticated remote sender could consume shared pairing capacity and trigger bounded relay/logging work on the Nostr channel. This issue did not grant message decryption, pairing approval, or broader authorization bypass.

## Affected Packages / Versions

- Package: `openclaw` (npm)
- Affected versions: `>= 2026.3.22, < 2026.3.31`
- Patched versions: `>= 2026.3.31`
- Latest published npm version: `2026.4.1`

## Fix Commit(s)

- `4ee742174f36b5445703e3b1ef2fbd6ae6700fa4` — verify inbound DM signatures before pairing replies

## Release Process Note

The fix shipped in OpenClaw `2026.3.31` on March 31, 2026. The current published npm release `2026.4.1` from April 1, 2026 also contains the fix.

Thanks @smaeljaish771 for reporting.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00253, "percentile": 0.16517}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-h43v-27wg-5mf9", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-07T18:14:39Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h43v-27wg-5mf9", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-h43v-27wg-5mf9"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-41301"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-21T00:16:30Z", "published_at": "2026-04-07T18:14:39Z", "references": ["https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/security/advisories/GHSA-h43v-27wg-5mf9", "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/4ee742174f36b5445703e3b1ef2fbd6ae6700fa4", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-41301", "https://www.vulncheck.com/advisories/openclaw-forged-nostr-dm-pairing-state-creation-via-signature-verification-bypass", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h43v-27wg-5mf9"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/security-advisories/GHSA-h43v-27wg-5mf9", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw", "summary": "OpenClaw: Forged Nostr DMs could create pairing state before signature verification", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T21:04:49Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-h43v-27wg-5mf9", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2026.3.31", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "openclaw"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 2026.3.22, < 2026.3.31"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: OpenClaw: Forged Nostr DMs could create pairing state before signature verification
